channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EU Confirms Andes Hantavirus on Cruise Ship Shows No Mutation
item.description: The European Union’s health authority, ECDC, has clarified that there is no evidence suggesting the Andes strain of hantavirus has mutated, following a recent outbreak on a cruise ship from Argentina ...

item.title: Reform UK consults parliamentary watchdog on Farage’s undeclared donation
item.description: Reform UK, the British anti-immigration party, is currently in talks with the parliamentary watchdog following reports that its leader, Nigel Farage, received a personal gift worth 5 million pounds (a...

item.title: The Journey of Skittles: From British Invention to Global Favorite
item.description: Skittles, often perceived as a quintessentially American candy, actually originated in Britain. Introduced in 1974 and named after a traditional British lawn game, Skittles spent its first five years ...

item.title: Starmer Urges Unity Amid Leadership Speculation and Ministerial Resignations
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has called on Labour ministers and MPs to avoid a leadership contest, warning it could 'plunge the party into chaos'. Facing internal pressure after four ministerial resignations and ...

item.title: Bato dela Rosa appears in Senate following shooting incident
item.description: Former Philippine National Police Chief and current Senator Bato dela Rosa was seen entering the Senate premises following a recent shooting incident. Although details of the incident have not been fu...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Ava Gardner in ‘The Little Hut’ (1957)
item.description: Ava Gardner captivated audiences with her timeless beauty and charm during the filming of the 1957 romantic comedy 'The Little Hut.' In the film, she portrayed Lady Susan Ashlow, an elegant and desira...

item.title: Georgia GOP Plans Redistricting That Could Target Black-Majority Districts Before 2028
item.description: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has announced a special legislative session to redraw congressional maps ahead of the 2028 elections. The move follows a recent Supreme Court ruling in Louisiana, which all...

item.title: 11 People Rescued After Plane Crashes into Waters Off Florida
item.description: On May 13, 2026, 11 individuals were rescued from the Atlantic Ocean following a plane crash roughly 80 kilometres off the coast of Melbourne, Florida. The aircraft, a Bahamian Beechcraft 300 King Air...

item.title: British Nursing Student Faces Court Summons in Greece Over Alleged 2022 Assault
item.description: A 24-year-old British nursing student, Sarah Honey, has been subpoenaed to return to Greece following an alleged sexual assault during a holiday in Crete in 2022. Despite her decision not to press cha...

item.title: The Story of the 'iPhone Girl' and Her Brief Viral Fame in 2008
item.description: In August 2008, shortly after the launch of the iPhone 3G, a British man named Mark Mitchell received a newly purchased iPhone that unexpectedly contained three photos of a young Chinese factory worke...

item.title: Henry Giroux on Resisting the Erosion of Empathy and Democratic Values Under Trump
item.description: In this opinion piece, author Henry A. Giroux argues that the Trump administration has accelerated a dangerous moral decline in American society. He contends that Trumpism thrives in a culture where c...

item.title: Trump Prepares Contingency Letter for Vice President JD Vance During China Visit
item.description: Senior Director for Counterterrorism Sebastian Gorka revealed that President Donald Trump has prepared a letter for Vice President JD Vance to be stored in the Resolute Desk, in the unlikely event tha...

item.title: Key Events in the Philippine Senate on May 13, 2026
item.description: On May 13, 2026, the Philippine Senate experienced a series of significant developments that attracted nationwide attention. The day included discussions on pending legislation, as well as a highly pu...

item.title: Trump Biographer Suggests Marco Rubio Could Be Fired for Outshining the President
item.description: According to Trump biographer Michael Wolff, President Donald Trump may be preparing to fire Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Wolff argues that Rubio's competence and consistent presence in the office ...

item.title: Canada Selected to Host NATO-Linked Defence Bank Amid Comprehensive Security Reforms
item.description: Canada has been chosen to host the Defence, Security and Resilience Bank (DSRB), a NATO-linked institution aimed at facilitating defence and security funding for both NATO and non-NATO countries. Pote...

item.title: Streamer claims self-defense in Nashville shooting following altercation over racial slurs
item.description: A Nashville-based livestreamer known as 'Chud the Builder,' whose real name is Dalton Eatherly, claimed he shot a Black man in self-defense after an altercation involving racial slurs. The incident oc...

item.title: Kenya Expands SHA Cancer Benefits and Treatment Services
item.description: The Kenyan Network of Cancer Organizations (KENCO) has applauded the government's decision to raise oncology benefits under the Social Health Authority (SHA) from Sh550,000 to Sh800,000. This increase...

item.title: Visa introduces AI tools to tackle rising payment disputes globally
item.description: Global payment giant Visa don introduce new AI-powered tools to help banks, merchants, and payment processors manage payment disputes better. According to Visa, the number of disputes dey rise for eve...

item.title: Sibanyoni and Co-accused’s Court Appearance Postponed Due to Magistrate Absence
item.description: Taxi boss Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni, 60, along with his co-accused Phillmon Msiza and Mvimbi Masilela, appeared in the Kwaggafontein magistrate’s court on charges of extortion and money laundering. The ...

item.title: Republicans Respond to Trump's Remarks About Americans' Financial Concerns
item.description: On May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump publicly stated that Americans' financial situations were not a consideration in his approach to ending the conflict with Iran. His comments, made outside the W...
